name: delon-acl
description: @delon/acl skill - Access Control List for role-based permissions and UI element visibility. For ng-events construction site progress tracking system.
capabilities: Generate ACL service code for setting user roles and abilities
Create route guards for protected Angular routes
Implement *aclIf directives for conditional UI rendering
Build permission checking logic with oneOf and allOf modes
author: 7Spade
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation and TypeScript code examples for @delon/acl library. All 52 static findings are false positives: hash values were misidentified as weak crypto, Angular dynamic imports and template literals as malicious scripts, and markdown syntax as shell commands. No executable code, network calls, or file system operations exist.
valueStatement: Angular applications need a reliable way to control user permissions and protect UI elements. This skill provides code patterns for implementing ACL with guards, directives, and permission checks.
limitations: Only provides documentation and code patterns for @delon/acl library
Does not install or configure the actual npm package
Requires Angular 17+ standalone components for best results

useCases.title: Build admin dashboards
useCases.description: Create admin interfaces with role-based UI elements and protected routes for different user levels.

useCases.title: Implement permission systems
useCases.description: Design and implement granular permission structures for multi-tenant Angular applications.

useCases.title: Connect UI to backend permissions
useCases.description: Bridge frontend ACL with backend security rules for comprehensive access control.

promptTemplates.title: Basic ACL setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to set up @delon/acl with ACLService to check user permissions in an Angular component.

promptTemplates.title: Route protection
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a CanActivateFn route guard using ACLService to protect admin routes in Angular.

promptTemplates.title: Conditional UI
promptTemplates.prompt: Show how to use *aclIf directive to conditionally show buttons based on user permissions.

promptTemplates.title: Blueprint permissions
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a PermissionService that integrates @delon/acl with ng-events blueprint permissions for construction site tracking.

bestPractices: Initialize ACL early using APP_INITIALIZER after authentication completes
Use computed signals with ACL for reactive permission checks
Combine client-side ACL with server-side security rules for defense in depth
antiPatterns: Hardcoding role checks directly in components instead of using ACLService
Relying only on client-side UI hiding without server-side validation
Not resetting ACL state during logout or user switching

faq.question: What Angular versions are supported?
faq.answer: @delon/acl supports Angular 17+ with standalone components and Angular 20+ with @if control flow.

faq.question: What permission formats does @delon/acl support?
faq.answer: It supports role-based (admin, user) and ability-based (task:create, blueprint:edit) permissions with oneOf/allOf modes.

faq.question: How does @delon/acl integrate with authentication?
faq.answer: Use @delon/auth for authentication then initialize ACLService with user roles in APP_INITIALIZER.

faq.question: Is client-side ACL secure enough?
faq.answer: No. Client-side ACL only hides UI elements. Always implement server-side validation for data access.

faq.question: Can I use ACL with Angular signals?
faq.answer: Yes. Create computed signals like: canEdit = computed(() => aclService.can('task:edit')).

faq.question: How is this different from other Angular permission libraries?
faq.answer: @delon/acl is part of the ng-alain ecosystem with built-in directives, guards, and deep Angular integration.
